title: Abort/Segfault: corrupted object freelist in `clear_freelist` (`object.c:909`)
description: `Objects/object.c`: per-PyThreadState object freelist `size` desyncs from its chain under OOM. GC's clear_freelist asserts `size==0||-1` (debug abort); the sibling surface is PyList_New popping a corrupted `lists` node with a dangling ob_item that free_list_items then frees (use-after-free SEGV). Root: PyList_New (listobject.c:248) pops a freelist list before the fallible list_allocate_array, and on OOM Py_DECREF(op) pushes it back, churning the freelist accounting.
notes: Free-threading-only abort surface (clear_freelist assert + per-PyThreadState freelists are Py_GIL_DISABLED). The DEBUG-only assert is the recorded vehicle signature; the reduced repro deterministically (start=4) hits the build-agnostic sibling SEGV (PyList_New -> free_list_items use-after-free of a 0xcd-filled freed array), which also crashes ft_release/jit/upstream. Matrix kept abort/n-a per brief (assert fires only on debug); underlying corruption is NOT debug-only. All 3 vehicles recorded the identical Objects/object.c:909 assertion; re-running them now non-deterministically yields the sibling SEGV. Distinct from the codeobject.c:2440 _co_unique_id assert the same vehicles can also hit (separate FT unique-id-pool invariant). 2026-06-21: the profiling_sampling_dump fleet vehicle (str.split via posixpath.relpath) reconfirms the sibling SEGV through ucs1lib_split_char->PyList_New; reproduced deterministically on ALL 6 local matrix builds at commit 1b9fe5c (debug/release x ft/gil x asan/jit), so the SEGV is build-agnostic and NOT FT-only (only the abort surface is). FT-asan leaf = free_list_items UAF of a 0xcd ob_item (start=6); GIL leaf = _Py_Dealloc on the corrupt popped list object one frame earlier (start=22). Alt str.split reproducer drafted (repro_split.py).
